title: BlackBerry to Report Q1 Earnings: What's in the Cards
text: For the quarter that ended May, analysts expected BlackBerry to lose 5 cents per share on revenue of $171.25 million, compared to the year-ago quarter when earnings came to 2 cents per share on revenue of $214.09 million. BlackBerry Limited was set to report first-quarter fiscal 2022 results on June 24, after the closing bell. Renewed interest in the so-called meme stock mania had sent BlackBerry shares surging some 377% from their March 2020 lows of $2.70 to a recent price of $13.41. While the company has carved out a leading position for itself in the fast-growing Enterprise of Things market, revenue has been difficult to come by.
